title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXXXVIII. Riddles.
content:
[A tobacco-pipe.]

        I went into my grandmother's garden,
        And there I found a farthing.
        I went into my next door neighbour's,
        There I bought a pipkin and a popkin - 
        A slipkin and a slopkin,
        A nailboard, a sailboard,
        And all for a farthing.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CLXXXVIII. Riddles."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
